          have h₁₀ : f x : Empty := f x
          exact isEmptyElim h₁₀
        exact h₆ h
      · intro h
        rw [h]
        exact ⟨fun x => Empty.elim x.val⟩
    have h₄ : Nonempty (I n → Empty) ↔ n = 0 := h₃ n
    have h₅ : Nonempty (I n → Empty) := by
      have h₆ : (I 0 : Type u_1) ∼ (I n : Type u_1) := h₂
      have h₇ : Nonempty (I 0 → Empty) := by
        exact ⟨fun x => Empty.elim x.val⟩
      have h₈ : Nonempty (I n → Empty) := by
        -- Use the equivalence to transfer the nonemptiness

        have h₁₀ : Nonempty (I n → Empty) := by
          -- Since I 0 is empty, any function from I 0 to Empty is nonempty

          have h₁₂ : Nonempty (I 0 → Empty) := h₉
          -- Use the fact that the equivalence preserves nonemptiness

            -- Construct a function from I n to Empty using the equivalence

            have h₁₄ : Nonempty (I 0 → Empty) := h₉

        exact h₁₀
      exact h₈
    have h₆ : n = 0 := by

      exact h₉
    exact h₆

  have h₂ : Fintype.card (I n) = n := by
    simp [I]
    <;> simp [Fintype.card_fin]
    <;> rfl
  
  have h₃ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := by
    have h₃₁ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := by
      apply Fintype.card_congr
      exact h
    exact h₃₁
  
  have h₄ : n = 0 := by
    have h₄₁ : Fintype.card (I n) = n := h₂
    have h₄₂ : Fintype.card (I 0) = 0 := h₁
    have h₄₃ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := h₃
    have h₄₄ : (0 : ℕ) = n := by
      calc
        (0 : ℕ) = Fintype.card (I 0) := by rw [h₁]
        _ = Fintype.card (I n) := by rw [h₃]
        _ = n := by rw [h₂]
    linarith
  
  apply h₄

0`.

   So the steps are:
   1. Prove that if `n > 0`, then `I n

by
  have h₁ : n = 0 := by
    by_contra hn
    -- Assume n ≠ 0 and derive a contradiction
    have h₂ : n ≠ 0 := hn
    have h₃ : n > 0 := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h₂
    -- I 0 is empty, and I n is non-empty for n > 0
    have h₄ : Nonempty (I 0) ↔ False := by
      constructor
      · intro h₅
        -- I 0 is empty, so this case is impossible
        rcases h₅ with ⟨x, hx⟩
        simp only [I, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx
        <;> omega
      · intro h₅
        -- False implies anything
        exfalso
        exact h₅
    have h₅ : Nonempty (I n) := by
      -- I n is non-empty for n > 0
      refine' ⟨0, _⟩
      simp only [I, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> omega
    -- I 0 and I n cannot be equinumerous if one is empty and the other is not
    have h₆ : ¬(I 0 ∼ I n) := by
      intro h₇
      have h₈ : Nonempty (I 0) ↔ Nonempty (I n) := by
        rw [Equiv.nonempty_iff_nonempty h₇]
      have h₉ : Nonempty (I 0) := by
        rw [h₈] at *
        exact h₅
      have h₁₀ : False := by
        have h₁₁ : Nonempty (I 0) ↔ False := h₄
        have h₁₂ : Nonempty (I 0) := h₉
        rw [h₁₁] at h₁₂
        exact h₁₂
      exact h₁₀
    -- Contradiction arises because h : I 0 ∼ I n and h₆ : ¬(I 0 ∼ I n)
    exact h₆ h
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : Fintype.card (I 0) = 0 := by simp [Fintype.card_fin]
  have h₂ : Fintype.card (I n) = n := by simp [Fintype.card_fin]
  have h₃ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := by

    <;> simp_all [Equiv]
    <;> aesop
  rw [h₁, h₂] at h₃
  <;> simp_all
  <;> aesop

by
  have h₁ : n = 0 := by
    by_contra hn
    have h₂ : n ≠ 0 := hn
    have h₃ : n > 0 := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h₂
    have h₄ : (∅ : Set ℕ) ∼ {x : ℕ | x < n} := by
      simpa [I, Set.ext_iff] using h
    have h₅ : ¬(∅ : Set ℕ) ∼ {x : ℕ | x < n} := by
      intro h₅
      have h₆ : Nonempty {x : ℕ | x < n} := by
        have h₇ : 0 < n := by linarith
        have h₈ : 0 < n := by linarith
        exact ⟨0, by simp [h₇]⟩
      have h₇ : Nonempty {x : ℕ | x < n} := h₆
      have h₈ : ¬Nonempty (∅ : Set ℕ) := by
        intro h₉
        obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h₉
        exact Set.not_mem_empty x hx
      have h₉ : Nonempty (∅ : Set ℕ) := by
        have h₁₀ : (∅ : Set ℕ) ∼ {x : ℕ | x < n} := h₅
        have h₁₁ : Nonempty {x : ℕ | x < n} := h₆
        have h₁₂ : Nonempty (∅ : Set ℕ) := by
          simpa [Set.ext_iff] using Set.nonempty_of_eq (Set.ext_iff.2 (Equiv.toFun h₁₀).symm) h₁₁
        exact h₁₂
      exact h₈ h₉
    exact h₅ h₄
  exact h₁

by
  classical
  have h₁ : n = 0 := by
    -- Use the fact that the interval [0, 0) is empty and compare the cardinality of the two intervals
    have h₂ : Nonempty (I 0) ↔ False := by
      constructor <;> intro h₃ <;>
      (try simp_all [I, empty, Nat.lt_succ_iff]) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try
        {
          obtain ⟨x⟩ := h₃
          simp [I, empty] at x
          <;> omega
        })
    have h₃ : Nonempty (I n) ↔ n ≠ 0 := by
      constructor <;> intro h₄ <;>
      (try cases n <;> simp_all [I, empty, Nat.lt_succ_iff]) <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try
        {
          obtain ⟨x⟩ := h₄
          simp [I, empty] at x
          <;> omega
        })
    have h₄ : I 0 ∼ I n := h
    have h₅ : Nonempty (I 0) ↔ Nonempty (I n) := by
      rw [h₄]
      <;>
      simp [Nonempty]
    have h₆ : ¬Nonempty (I 0) := by
      simp [h₂]
    have h₇ : ¬Nonempty (I n) := by
      intro h₈
      have h₉ : Nonempty (I n) := h₈
      have h₁₀ : Nonempty (I 0) := by
        simp_all [h₅]
      contradiction
    have h₈ : n = 0 := by
      by_contra h₉
      have h₁₀ : n ≠ 0 := h₉
      have h₁₁ : Nonempty (I n) := by
        have h₁₂ : Nonempty (I n) := by
          have h₁₃ : n ≠ 0 := h₁₀
          have h₁₄ : Nonempty (I n) := by
            rw [h₃]
            simp [h₁₃]
          exact h₁₄
        exact h₁₂
      contradiction
    exact h₈
  exact h₁

  have h₁ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := by

    <;>
    (try omega) <;>
    (try simp_all [I]) <;>
    (try omega)
    <;>
    (try
      {
        cases n <;> simp_all [I, Nat.succ_eq_add_one]
        <;> omega
      })
  
  exact h₂

by
  have h₁ : IsEmpty (I 0) := inferInstance
  have h₂ : IsEmpty (I n) := by sorry
  have h₃ : n = 0 := by sorry
  sorry

by
  have h₁ : n = 0 := by
    -- Since I 0 is empty, the only possibility for I n to be equinumerous with it is if I n is also empty.
    -- But I n is empty only when n = 0.
    have h₂ : n = 0 := by
      by_contra hn
      -- If n ≠ 0, then I n is non-empty because it contains 0.
      have h₃ : Nonempty (I n) := ⟨⟨0, by
        -- Prove that 0 < n when n ≠ 0.
        have h₄ : n ≠ 0 := hn
        have h₅ : 0 < n := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h₄
        omega⟩⟩
      -- But I 0 is empty, and we have a contradiction because non-empty sets cannot be equinumerous with empty sets.
      have h₄ : ¬Nonempty (I 0) := by simp [I]
      have h₅ : Nonempty (I 0) := by
        -- This is a contradiction because I 0 is empty.
        have h₆ : I 0 ∼ I n := h
        have h₇ : Nonempty (I n) := h₃
        have h₈ : Nonempty (I 0) := by
          -- Use the fact that I 0 ∼ I n and I n is non-empty to show I 0 is non-empty.
          exact Nonempty.ofEquiv h₆ h₇
        exact h₈
      exact h₄ h₅
    exact h₂
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : Fintype.card (I 0) = 0 := by
    simp [Fintype.card_subtype]
    <;> decide
  have h₂ : Fintype.card (I n) = n := by
    simp [Fintype.card_subtype]
    <;> simp_all [Finset.Icc_eq_empty]
    <;> decide
  have h₃ : Fintype.card (I 0) = Fintype.card (I n) := by
    apply Fintype.card_congr h
  rw [h₁] at h₃
  have h₄ : n = 0 := by
    omega
  exact h₄

by
  have h₁ : I 0 = ∅ := by
    -- The interval [0, 0) is the empty set.
    simp [I]
    <;> aesop
  -- Simplify the equivalence using the fact that I 0 is empty.
  have h₂ : I 0 ∼ I n := h
  rw [h₁] at h₂
  -- The empty set is equivalent only to the empty set.
  have h₃ : (∅ : Set ℕ) ∼ I n := h₂
  have h₄ : (I n : Set ℕ) = ∅ := by
    -- Since the empty set is equivalent to I n, I n must also be empty.
    apply Set.eq_empty_of_forall_not_mem
    intro x hx
    -- If there were an element in I n, it would contradict the equivalence.
    have h₅ : (∅ : Set ℕ) ∼ I n := h₃
    have h₆ : Nonempty (I n : Set ℕ) := ⟨x, hx⟩
    have h₇ : Nonempty (∅ : Set ℕ) := by
      -- The empty set cannot be nonempty, leading to a contradiction.
      exact Nonempty.ofEquiv h₅.symm h₆
    simp at h₇
  -- Since I n is empty, n must be 0.
  have h₅ : n = 0 := by
    have h₆ : (I n : Set ℕ) = ∅ := h₄
    have h₇ : n = 0 := by
      by_contra h₈
      -- If n were not 0, then I n would contain 0, contradicting its emptiness.
      have h₉ : 0 ∈ (I n : Set ℕ) := by
        simp [I, h₈]
        <;> omega
      rw [h₆] at h₉
      simp at h₉
    exact h₇
  exact h₅

by
  have h₁ : n = 0 := by
    have h₂ : (I 0 : Set ℕ) = ∅ := by
      ext x
      simp [I]
      <;> omega
    have h₃ : (I n : Set ℕ) = (I n : Set ℕ) := rfl
    have h₄ : (I 0 : Set ℕ) ∼ (I n : Set ℕ) := by simpa [h₂, h₃] using h
    have h₅ : (I n : Set ℕ) = ∅ ↔ n = 0 := by
      constructor
      · intro h₆
        by_cases hn : n = 0
        · exact hn
        · -- If n ≠ 0, then I n is not empty because 0 ∈ I n
          have h₇ : 0 < n := Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hn
          have h₈ : 0 ∈ (I n : Set ℕ) := by
            simp [I]
            <;> omega
          have h₉ : (I n : Set ℕ) ≠ ∅ := by
            intro h₁₀
            rw [h₁₀] at h₈
            exact Set.not_mem_empty _ h₈
          exact absurd h₆ h₉
      · intro h₆
        rw [h₆]
        simp [I]
        <;> ext x <;> simp [I] <;> omega
    have h₆ : (I n : Set ℕ) = ∅ := by
      have h₇ : (I 0 : Set ℕ) = ∅ := by
        ext x
        simp [I]
        <;> omega
      have h₈ : (I n : Set ℕ) ≈ (I 0 : Set ℕ) := by
        simpa [h₇] using h₄.symm
      have h₉ : (I n : Set ℕ) ≈ (∅ : Set ℕ) := by
        simpa [h₇] using h₈
      have h₁₀ : (I n : Set ℕ) = ∅ := by
        simpa [Set.eq_empty_iff_forall_not_mem] using Set.equiv_empty_iff.mp h₉
      exact h₁₀
    have h₇ : n = 0 := (h₅.mp h₆)
    exact h₇
  exact h₁
